programme.title: Weird or What? With William Shatner: Power of the Mind
programme.desc: A housewife who believes a psychic vision helped her solve a murder, and a man who remembers every moment of his life with pinpoint accuracy. Plus, a healer who claims to cure diseases through prayer

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Man Beasts
programme.desc: Man Beasts: We're all fascinated by mysteries. But is the unexplained really unexplainable? William Shatner investigates in an attempt to find a logical explanation.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Curses
programme.desc: Curses: Do cursed objects really exist? This episode explores a painting in Britain that is said to start deadly house fires, a sinister doll in Florida and a curse-proof house.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Aliens Walk Among Us
programme.desc: Stories that suggest aliens are among us, including an Arizona woman who claims to have been fathered by an alien in a top-secret government experiment.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Warning

programme.title: Dynasty: The Cry

programme.title: Dynasty: The Rescue

programme.title: Dynasty: The Triple-Cross

programme.title: Dynasty: The Vendetta
programme.desc: Blake attacks Alexis when she attempts to take over the mansion; an emotionally disturbed Claudia is trapped by a wall of flame; Sammy Jo and Amanda fight for Clay's affections; Dominique learns the truth about Garrett's first marriage.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: After months of intimate texts with Titus, a man she met online, Candice has decided to tell to her husband. But before she does, she asks Nev to help her find out who Titus really is! (S5, ep16)

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Fans of popular YouTube star Andrea Russett call on Nev and Max to help catch a potential stalker who is impersonating Andrea's family online. What the guys uncover will shock even them. (S5, ep17)

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Long Islander Catherine has fallen in love with Graham from Tennessee. She's ready to uproot her life to be with him, but Nev and Max discover that Graham's not everything he claims to be. (S5, ep18)

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team struggles to explain how a DAF 33 gearbox works, and office manager Sarah attempts to bring order to the auto memorabilia sale.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave finds a very rare 70s Sunbeam Lotus that has been rotting away in a farmyard for 29 years, while a Morris Minor convertible goes under the hammer.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A classic car fan with a love of the Jaguar E-Type reluctantly sends his dream car to auction, hoping to buy a motorhome. Two Ford Escorts get Dave's pulse racing.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The blue lights are flashing as crews attend a cycling accident and a man having a suspected stroke. Plus, the paramedics assist a woman who has fallen and a baby with multiple seizures.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics cope with a reported stabbing and help a man who was run over by his own car. They also visit a woman suffering from a panic attack and attend a man in agony from a dislocated hip.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a pensioner who is suffering from a head injury and a 2-year-old who's had a seizure. Elsewhere, crews treat a man with abdominal pain and take a man with COVID-19 to hospital.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The crews attend a man with an unexplained broken leg, a boy with pneumonia and a woman with breathing problems. They also help a man who has fallen under the wheels of his own car.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Oxford, the crew help a child with signs of meningitis and take an injured cyclist to hospital. In Portsmouth, they help a man who has fallen and a patient with Crohn's Disease.

programme.title: The Maury Show: 4 Women… 4 Babies… Is He the Father?
programme.desc: We have more of the paternity pandemonium that you crave! You’ll see denying dads, mad moms and some of the most shocking results we’ve ever seen! Don’t miss one second of the drama on today’s MAURY!

programme.desc: Toni, Quintin, and his mom return to find out who fathered his son. This time they’ll wonder if Toni cheated! Jenny needs to know whether her husband Matt or his friend fathered her child! Finally, will Vivian end her 30 year search for her son?

programme.title: The Maury Show: DNA Tests Will Prove You Have 10 Kids!

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: ANCIENT MEGASTRUCTURES
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at ancient megastructures including the Olmec Heads of Central America, Moai of Eastern Island, Petra in modern day Jordan, the Alhambra in Spain, Angkor Wat in Cambodia, Chichen Itza.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: NATURE’S MAGIC
programme.desc: Natural sites worldwide hold spiritual significance for civilizations, from ancient rituals to modern reverence, each crackling with unique energy and a hint of magic.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: PEOPLE WITHOUT IDENTITY
programme.desc: 10 Mysterious Figures: Individuals who emerged from obscurity, made their mark on history, then vanished without a trace. Their identities remain unconfirmed, shrouding them in mystery.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS WORKS OF ART
programme.desc: Great art and its creators unveil mysteries more intricate than TV dramas. Amidst theft and intrigue, we persist in solving them. Yet, the truth often lies buried with those who created it, adding layers of intrigue to the art world.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: UNSOLVED MYSTERIES IN PHYSICS
programme.desc: In the late 19th century, scientists thought all major physics laws were discovered. Now, we realize vast unknowns remain. Despite our understanding, mysteries persist in the physical world.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS CREATURES
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries examines mysterious creatures that may live in Earth including the chupacabra, the Loch Ness Monster, the thunderbird,) the yeti or Bigfoot.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: UNEXPLAINED MESSAGES
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at mysterious unexplained messages from the past including the Nazca Lines, the Voynich Manuscript, the Flower of Life, Puma Punku, Leonardo Da Vinci's paintings.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS AND UNSOLVED DISSAPEARANCES
programme.desc: Legally declared dead, bodies never found. Disappearances spark speculation, media attention, and unanswered questions. Where did they go?

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: University students Holly Dunn and Chris Maier are ambushed along railway tracks in August 1997. By 1999, a string of similar attacks leads police to Angel Maturino Reséndiz, who travels by freight train between murders across three states.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2004, Marc Dutroux was sentenced to life for kidnapping, raping, and murdering young girls in Belgium. His 1996 arrest and delayed trial exposed horrific crimes and official failings, sparking public outrage.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 28, 2005, Joan Porco attends the trial of her son Christopher, accused of murdering his father and attacking her. Despite her initial identification and later refusal to testify, Christopher is found guilty based on circumstantial evidence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: Murder by the Mob depicts the battle for justice by Peppino Impastato's family after his 1978 death was misclassified. His mother and brother reopened the case, leading to mafia boss Gaetano Badalamenti's 2002 conviction for the murder.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, Ashley Pond and Miranda Gaddis disappear in Portland. Neighbor Ward Weaver, a suspect with a violent history, is later found guilty of their murders and sentenced to life in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: A picture-perfect Canadian couple Karla Homolka and Paul Bernardo join to perform torture and mutilation on young victims - crimes that are virtually unspeakable. Two teenage girls were raped and murdered, and the couple even filmed themselves doing it.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In July 2000, Sarah Payne is abducted and murdered. Roy Whiting, a convicted sex offender, is found guilty and sentenced to life in 2001. Sarah’s case leads to the implementation of “Sarah’s Law” in the UK in 2011.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In September 1987, Simon Dale is murdered. His ex-wife, Susan Wilberforce, is acquitted of the crime but later convicted of fraud and sentenced to seven years. The murder remains unsolved.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, fashion journalist Christa Worthington is found murdered in her Truro home. Christopher McCowen is convicted in 2006 of her murder, robbery, and rape, despite his claims of innocence and other DNA found at the scene.

programme.title: Naked Science: Diamonds
programme.desc: Naked Science follows the story of a diamond from the creation of carbon inside the heart of a dying sun, through to the cutting of a diamond mine in South Africa.

programme.title: Naked Science: Jupiter
programme.desc: By far the largest planet, 1300 times the size of Earth, it’s a world so enormous it could swallow every planet and moon in the solar system and still have room to spare.

programme.title: Naked Science: Super Flares - Could They Cause Mass Extinction?
programme.desc: Superflares are amongst the greatest events of the Universe. These magnetic explosions can release energy millions of times greater than atomic bombs. They produce streams of highly energetic particles which can be highly destructive to life.

programme.title: Naked Science: Mars
programme.desc: Mars our closest neighbor now a lifeless planet – but it may once have had water and therefore life. Naked Science investigates the red planet to learn how it was formed, how it became barren, and ask whether it ever will be possible to colonize it?

programme.title: Naked Science: The Great Flood
programme.desc: Almost every culture on Earth tells the story of an ancient flood – details vary but the basic plot is the same - An angry God wipes out an unrepentant population.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Return To The Moon
programme.desc: The Moon, our closest celestial companion. Our indispensible dance partner through the cosmos.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Inner Worlds
programme.desc: They orbit between us and our Star, the inner worlds; hidden within the sun's glare often only visible at sunrise or sunset.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Next Stop Mars
programme.desc: It has fired our imaginations for millennia, Mars, the God of War and the source of Man's science fictional demise.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Planet Makers
programme.desc: From the beginnings of our Solar System four and half billion years ago there remains tantalizing clues to its evolution; remnent debris: asteroids and comets.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Ice Giants
programme.desc: Beyond the asteroid belt lay the ice giants, some can be seen with the naked eye, others only glimpsed once by a passing probe.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Moons of Olympis
programme.desc: These are the the Moon's of the gas giants. Each a unique and mysterious world of its own. Some have oceans of water, geysers of Sulphur or atmospheres of plastic.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Sun
programme.desc: It powers the machinery of nature; our weather, encourages and sustains life on land and at sea.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Exo Planeta
programme.desc: As scientists and astronomers peer closer at nearby stars with ever more fidelity they are discovering planets; exo planets, worlds outside our own solar system.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Edge of The Universe
programme.desc: The night sky is a time machine. The further we look out into the universe the further back in time we reach.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Astronauts
programme.desc: One hundred kilometres above the Earth’s surface lies an invisible frontier: the edge of space.

programme.title: Wildest Middle East
programme.desc: Wildest Middle East reveals the remotest and most surprising corners of this ancient land, where you will find nothing is as it seems and where the natural world is fighting a constant battle to survive in an ever changing world.

programme.title: Meerkat Manor: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Zorro takes a group of Whiskers boys off roving at the Commandos.

programme.title: Wildest Arctic: Iceland: Land of Fire and Ice
programme.desc: Iceland is a land born out of fire, sculpted by ice. This unique island is home to some of the planet's rarest and most spectacular creatures and it harbors some of the most extreme environments on Earth.

programme.title: Wild Assassins: Tusk and Horns
programme.desc: Welcome to the world of wild assassins, where creatures with weapons carved from bone and ivory stalk the open plains.

programme.title: Wild Asia: The Arid Heart
programme.desc: Staying alive in Asia’s barren heartland of desert and dry grasslands is a challenge, yet many animals thrive.

programme.title: Wildest Arctic: Taiga: The Frozen Forest
programme.desc: Containing one third of the world's trees, the frozen forests of the Taiga are the lungs of the planet. This great forest spans across shores of the Pacific, sheltering some of the Earth's most majestic animals, and some of its most secretive.

programme.title: Warship: Life at Sea: Episode 1

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown: Thailand
programme.desc: Tony teams up with Chef and Author Andy Ricker of Pok Pok Restaurant fame. He's made a name for himself faithfully reproducing the cuisine of Northern Thailand at his restaurants in Portland and NYC.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Tony explores what Burroughs called "Interzone," where artists sought escape from Western moral prohibitions and the possibilities of great empty spaces. But what is it like now?

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Tony visits Andalucia during Semana Santa – Holy Week – a time filled with great pageantry and excitement. Tony’s longtime DP, Zach Zamboni, currently lives in Granada with his fiancée, Fuen Sanchez.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: Arthur: No Acting Please/Prunella Deegan and the Disappointing Ending
programme.desc: When Fern lands a role in a community production, she fears she's not talented enough to be a star. Finally, the final volume of the Henry Screever series is out. Prunella and Marina compete to see who can finish the book the fastest.

programme.title: Arthur: Citizen Frensky/D.W.'S Backpack Mishap
programme.desc: After visiting The Elwood City Times, Francine decides to start her own paper--The Frensky Star! The tabloid is a success--until all her friends stop speaking to her. Somebody stole D.W.'s backpack!! Who is this fluffy unicorn thief?

programme.title: Arthur: Tales of Grotesquely Grim Bunny/Pet Projects
programme.desc: When the comic book store changes owners, the new darker comics are right up Fern's alley, but Arthur can't seem to stomach them. Arthur and Francine enter their pets to compete against each other in the "Perfect Pet" video contest.

programme.title: Arthur: I'm A Poet/The Scare-Your-Pants-Off Club
programme.desc: Challenged by Fern to enter a poetry contest, Arthur and his friends each write a poem to be judged by the famous poet Jack Prelutsky. Then, Arthur and his friends develop a consuming passion for the Scare-Your-Pants-Off books.

programme.title: Arthur: Follow the Bouncing Ball/Buster Baxter and the Letter from the Sea
programme.desc: Alberto Molina's beloved soccer ball--signed by his favorite Ecuadorian soccer star --is lost! While on vacation, Buster discovers a message in a bottle on the shore. Could it be a message from the people of the lost city of Atlantis?

programme.title: Arthur: Show Off/Dog's Best Friend
programme.desc: It's the Elwood City dog show and Killer is facing off against Sebastian Winkleplotz, the snooty, championship-winning poodle. Arthur agrees to watch Amigo while Alberto's off at camp, but Pal gets worried.

programme.title: Arthur: Mr. Ratburn's Secret Identity/Besties
programme.desc: Mysterious calls. Superhuman strength. Is Mr. Ratburn secretly a superhero? When Buster and Ladonna bond over a memorable trip to the movies, Arthur feels left out. Has Ladonna taken his place as Buster's best bud?

programme.title: Arthur: D.W. Goes To Washington/Arthur's Mystery Envelope
programme.desc: D.W.'s on her way to Washington and she's bored by it all until she gets lost during a tour of the White House. Then, when Mr. Haney gives Arthur a sealed envelope to bring home to Mom, Arthur fears the worst.

programme.title: NEW: Great Tank Battles of World War II
programme.desc: The third episode opens with Rommel’s second offensive in North Africa.

programme.title: NEW: Great Tank Battles of World War II
programme.desc: The fourth episode sees the Germans well and truly on the retreat and the Allies are able to successfully implement operation Husky: the landing on Sicily.

programme.title: RAF: The Royal Air Force: Battle for the Skies
programme.desc: The series begins before WWI when military commanders struggled to understand what uses aviation might have. The Wright Brothers had been the first to fly a powered aircraft in controlled flight in 1903, but even by 1914 aircraft were still underpowered.

programme.title: RAF: The Royal Air Force: Battle for the Skies
programme.desc: The 1920s and 30s were uncertain times for the RAF as well as all other armed services. With peace came cuts in spending on new aircraft development.

programme.title: RAF: The Royal Air Force: Battle for the Skies
programme.desc: By the end of the war the RAF had aircraft that could escort bombers to targets deep inside Germany and back. With the Gloster Meteor, the RAF had also entered the ‘Jet Age’. Aircraft like the Hawker Hunter and F-86 Sabre took the RAF supersonic.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Expeditions: Cro Magnon
programme.desc: The debate about humanity's true origins becomes a nefarious fixation as SS leader Heinrich Himmler turns his attention to proving the Paleolithic past of Germany's Aryan race.

programme.title: Bomber:Terror of WWII: The Means To Victory
programme.desc: The Means to Victory: The story of a deadly war that intertwines Germany, Poland, Japan, Italy and Spain as the remorseless development of weapons and bombers.

programme.title: History's Greatest Aircraft
programme.desc: B17: The mighty Flying Fortress takes to the air as we examine the background and development of one of WWII's most iconic bombers.

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: The elite German submariners who caused havoc in the heart of the American desert, the playboy pilot they couldn’t cage, and the young lovers whose escape defied all odds.

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Discover the true story of a German Luftwaffe pilot in World War II who risked his own life to save the crew of an American B-17 bomber.

programme.title: Hippo Supremacy
programme.desc: Witness the harsh realities that shape hippo life on Zambia’s Luangwa River.

programme.title: Wildlife Icons
programme.desc: The food chain of Africa’s savannah is a complex web of entangled wildlife.

programme.title: Wildlife Icons
programme.desc: Africa’s antelope have rich stories filled with daily struggles of survival.

programme.title: Australia's Hidden Islands
programme.desc: Species found nowhere else make a tiny island a living laboratory of evolution.

programme.title: Australia's Hidden Islands
programme.desc: Shifting sands form a perpetually evolving island full of curious habitats.

programme.title: Arabian Inferno

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: In a casing that fell from a circus caravan, the Lemmings find a magic stick that can enchant objects and make them come alive.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

programme.desc: On today's Steve Wilkos, will the lie detector results reveal if Tamisha and Simone's men were faithful? Or will it reveal some more sinister results?

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: Steve Outraged!
programme.desc: Lagraciala tragically lost her ten month old son and cremated him, but after a serious fight with her fiancée AJ, she couldn't find the ashes. AJ admits to threatening to steal the ashes, but denies he took them!

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: These DNA Results Will Floor You
programme.desc: Joe met “Jessica” on a phone chat line. A few months later, she revealed she was pregnant. Then, Chastity claims that her ex Trent fathered her child. What will the DNA results reveal?

programme.title: The Jerry Springer Show: Hot-headed Hotties!
programme.desc: Salina is seething over her sister Jessica sleeping with her fiancé.

programme.title: The Jerry Springer Show: Three Sisters Share A Man!
programme.desc: Kenny lived with sisters Lora and Misty, who are also his lovers but he treats them like servants. Later, Jerry confronts Kenny with the results of the nine positive paternity tests, but Kenny says he only cares about two of his nine children.

programme.title: The Jerry Springer Show: Stay Out of My Threesome!
programme.desc: Nicole wants her boyfriend back but only if he'll end his threesome. Next, Mesha's been in love with her husband's best friend, Jamie, for four years, but she won't take "No" for an answer.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes Faces Death
programme.desc: During WWII, several murders occur at a convalescent home where Dr. Watson has volunteered his services. Holmes is summoned to solve the crime from a long list of suspects including the owners, staff, and the patients.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: The Case of the Eiffel Tower
programme.desc: After a famous criminal is run over and killed by a milk wagon, Insp. Lestrade finds a mysterious coded note in the man's clothing.

programme.title: The Hound of the Baskervilles
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes and Dr Watson investigate the legend of a supernatural hound, a beast that may be stalking a young heir on the fog-shrouded moorland that makes up his estate.

programme.title: Sherlock: A Case of Evil
programme.desc: This tale focuses on the young 20-something Sherlock Holmes. Sherlock is in pursuit of the evil Moriarty and apparently shoots him to death, but his body cannot be found as he falls in a sewer.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: This time, the chefs compete in a special lunch service in honour of two charities run by Los Angeles firefighters, Nick surprises everyone when he bumps into an ex-girlfriend, and further tensions emerge during dinner service.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: This time, the chefs must prepare a Greek meal each choosing a protein as a basis for their dish. Meanwhile, Josh gives himself a motivational speech after being singled out for his lack of leadership.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Today, the chefs must create a dish using an alcohol - either beer, wine or a spirit. Judging the dishes along with Gordon are a couple of guest bartenders. Meanwhile, a slipped disc threatens Bret's progress.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Today, the chefs are faced with a selection of exotic protein foods and have to try to grab what they want to use for their dish, including alligator, wild boar and ostrich.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Today's challenge is to prepare a homecoming dinner for the Calabasas High School with the theme of Mardi Gras using seafood, chicken and beef.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Stone Cold
programme.desc: In 2004, Brendan Percy was found dead of hypothermia and dehydration in a sub alpine area above Oamaru in suspicious circumstances.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: A Bump in the Night
programme.desc: In 1980, six-year-old Alicia O'Reilly was raped and murdered in her bed, while her sister slept less than a metre away, and her mother sleeping across the hallway.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Out of Aces
programme.desc: Garth Doull trained to be a policeman but the lure of the race track saw him swap sides of the law to become a consummate con-man. On the 8th of September 1989, his body was found floating in Wellington harbour.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Lost Soul
programme.desc: In 1985, 25-year-old Luana 'La Verne' Williams disappeared from her Tauranga home. Eight years later her disappearance was upgraged to a murder enquiry. Her body has never been found.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Walk of Innocence
programme.desc: In 1970, eighteen-year-old Olive Waiker disappeared as she walked across Rotorua to babysit her nieces and nephews. Her semi-naked body was found hours later in a rest area south of the city..

programme.title: Ghost Hunters: A Serial Killer's Revenge
programme.desc: Jason and the TAPS team head to Charleston, South Carolina, one of the most haunted cities in America and winner of Ghost Hunters America's Hometown Ghost Hunt Contest.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters: Camp Fear
programme.desc: On this episode of Ghost Hunters, Jason and TAPS go to camp! They head to Camp Rutledge in Georgia where a woman has experienced paranormal activity since she was a teenager.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters: French Quarter Massacre
programme.desc: On this episode of Ghost Hunters, TAPS heads out to New Orleans, Louisiana to investigate their first case without Grant: The Jimani Lounge Massacre.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters: Don't Feed the Apparition
programme.desc: On this episode of Ghost Hunters, TAPS revisits a familiar city, Alexandria, Louisiana, but this time, they're there to investigate the Alexandria Zoo.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters: Ghostly Refuge
programme.desc: On this episode of Ghost Hunters, TAPS heads to Oswego, New York to investigate the renowned Fort Ontario. TAPS has investigated many forts, but never one that once served as a sanctuary for Jewish refugees during World War II.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters: Paranormal Politics
programme.desc: On this episode of Ghost Hunters, TAPS heads down South to help two different families find some closure. First stop is Baton Rouge, Louisiana to investigate the Old State Capital Building, which has endured theCivil War and a congressman's death.

programme.title: NEW The Parkers: J.c. Bowl
programme.desc: A scholastic competition sparks a new love in Kim’s life and an old rivalry from Professor Oglevee’s past.

programme.title: NEW The Parkers: Whassup With Heyyy?
programme.desc: After T launches a successful business using one of Kim and Nikki’s favorite sayings, all three of them wind up in court.

programme.title: NEW The Parkers: Scary Kim
programme.desc: The Grim Reaper turns Kim’s birthday bash into the ultimate fright fest.

programme.title: NEW The Parkers: Election 2000
programme.desc: Nikki and Kim square off in a campus election.

programme.title: NEW The Parkers: Heir Today, Gone Tomorrow
programme.desc: Kim puts her own spin on the legendary television show, 'Dallas,' by imagining her family and friends as the twisted characters from the primetime series of lust and greed.

programme.title: Tyler Perry's Sistas: The Sister Circle
programme.desc: Sabrina tries to navigate the waters of friendship despite some clashing personalities. Andi's greatest secret is revealed. (S1, ep11)

programme.title: Tyler Perry's Sistas: All I Got
programme.desc: Gary's secrets are revealed, putting him and Andi at odds once more. The shadow of Aaron's past overwhelms Karen. Danni tries to be there for Zac. (S1, ep12)

programme.desc: Karen's world is rocked when blood is on her hands. Sabrina is in for a big surprise with one of Maurice's plans. Danni plays "middle-man" to some troubling news. (S1, ep13)

programme.title: Paranormal State: Vegas
programme.desc: The PRS travels to Sin City to investigate a 14-year old medium who claims to speak to a dead girl.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Messenger
programme.desc: Kelli and her husband Riley are frightened in their coastal Oregon home. They are plagued by a headless apparition and often hear mysterious noises that resemble cries for help from the dark corners of their house.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Suicide Possession
programme.desc: Joan and her boyfriend Tony are seeing apparitions in their New Jersey home. Both are concerned that the spirits are influencing their behavior: a non-drinker, Tony has turned to the bottle while Joan has thoughts of harming herself.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Anniversary
programme.desc: Ever since a close-knit Kentucky family moved into their new home, they've been visited by the spirit of a young girl.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Hell's Gate
programme.desc: Ever since Wes and his family moved into their new home in York, PA they've been struggling with a dark force that's made everyday a living hell. Wes feels his mental and physical health is in jeopardy.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Glove
programme.desc: When a terrifying haunting threatens to break a Daisytown, PA couple apart, PRS is their last resort. As the investigation deepens, PRS and the family learn that it's just the tip of iceberg, turns out this haunting might be all in the family.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Darkness Falls
programme.desc: Ryan leads his team to one of the most haunted places in the US to confront their fears and solve a paranormal mystery. Aside from the alleged spirits of dead prisoners, something even darker may be lurking inside West Virginia State Penitentiary.

programme.title: Paranormal State: School House Haunting
programme.desc: PRS travels to Massachusetts to investigate an old schoolhouse where children's voices disturb a couple who've made it their home.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Hostage to Fear
programme.desc: P.R.S. is summoned to help a family who has been haunted for years by spirits lurking in the house. Upon arrival the team finds a family that has become prisoners in their own home.

programme.title: Most Haunted S24
programme.desc: Yvette starts the new series with an investigation at the haunted Eden Camp. Strange noises and an unknown force try to separate the team. Can they endure the entire investigation, or will the paranormal activity prove too much for them to handle?

programme.title: Most Haunted S24
programme.desc: Yvette and her world-famous team continue investigating Eden Camp. Splitting into lone vigils, they quickly regret it as lost souls, dark shadows, and disembodied voices haunt this living museum, all eager to make their presence known.

programme.title: Most Haunted S24
programme.desc: The Most Haunted team visit this deserted old mansion which is way past its original splendour. They are soon surrounded by unexplained activity as something is following them and for at least two members of crew, they are pushed to their very limits.

programme.title: Most Haunted S24
programme.desc: An all but deserted manor house that boasts murder, death and intrigue among its history becomes the setting for one of the most intriguing investigation to date. The team are showered with object that appear to land in the same way.

programme.title: Most Haunted S24
programme.desc: Yvette and team are put to the test as activity seems to be all around them, something is following them but who or what. Could it be the spirit of a man they connect who died near the house over 100 years ago?

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Louisiana Purchase
programme.desc: In Louisiana, Ton and Allen encounter some salty locals at a shipping crate auction, and navigate choppy waters at a boat auction. After spending a lot of cash, they find a tactical blast shield and a bass fishing boat.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Sidecar Surprise
programme.desc: An unassuming mom in an oversized sweater tries to pull the wool over Ton & Allen's eyes at a storage auction in Arlington, VA. The guys uncover a black powder cannon designed in the 15th century & a Russian military motorcycle!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Drumming Up Cash
programme.desc: Ton & Allen face some silent but deadly competition at an auction in nearby Glendale. They discover a Zildjian brand percussion cymbal from the 1940's and a pyrotechnics control board designed for large-scale fireworks displays!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Viva la Vegas
programme.desc: 1974 death-defying Zig Zag magic sword box, an ultra-rare 1910 poster of the infamous magician Chung Ling Soo, an 1800s naval cane sword and century-old Metlach German beer steins.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Quadzilla Attacks!
programme.desc: Extreme Quadzilla ATV, the fastest 4 wheeler ever; a 1910s Remington Derringer and an 1880s Palm Squeezer pistol; revolutionary 1800s Western Electric telephone with a crank dial.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Disco and Dice
programme.desc: 1978 Williams Disco pinball machine; 1898 dice popper gambling device; talking 1990s bar top breathalyzer; deadly fishing harpoon.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Once a Pawn a Time
programme.desc: Ton and Allen discover that owning a pawn shop is way more trouble than they expected. With bills stacking up, even an antique ammo collection and some classic football helmets may not be enough to put the guys in the black.

programme.title: Criminal Confessions
programme.desc: When a single mother with a history of heart problems is found dead, detectives assume she died of natural causes. But as the investigation deepens, detectives begin to believe foul play was involved.

programme.title: Criminal Confessions
programme.desc: Almost a decade after Californian student Lynsie Ekelund went missing, detectives call the main suspect's bluff when they find human remains at the construction site he worked at.

programme.title: Criminal Confessions

programme.title: Criminal Confessions

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS AND UNSOLVED DISSAPEARANCES
programme.desc: Legally declared dead, bodies never found. Disappearances spark speculation, media attention, and unanswered questions. Where did they go?

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SUPRESSED INVENTIONS IN HISTORY
programme.desc: Explore lost or suppressed inventions from history, some skeptical, others mysterious. Despite doubts, many are useful today. Stay open-minded as we delve into these intriguing relics.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET PROJECTS AND OPERATIONS
programme.desc: Imagine a world with open use of resources from secret government projects. Little is known about these black ops. They hold mysteries and consequences, challenging democratic ideals. History reveals human experimentation, contrary to official claims.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SCIENCE AND MYSTERIES FOR 21. CENTURY
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at some of the mysteries of modern science including memory of water, reincarnation, the beginning of life, hypnosis, the secrets of Morphic fields, time travel, why we must sleep.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: In 1938, Nazi Germany annexed Austria. Hitler seized the Spear of Destiny, a Roman spearhead believed to hold power from Christ's crucifixion. He paraded it in Nuremberg to assert authority, but by 1945, amid Germany's ruin, the spear had vanished.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: It’s one of the most powerful and enduring myths ever created. The Garden of Eden. One maverick scholar, Dr Juris Zarins sets out to prove its existence.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: Forensic pathologist Dr Philippe Charlier, known to the French as the “Indiana Jones of the graveyards” faces his biggest challenge yet: to uncover the truth behind the remains of France’s most famous daughter – Joan of Arc.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, fashion journalist Christa Worthington is found murdered in her Truro home. Christopher McCowen is convicted in 2006 of her murder, robbery, and rape, despite his claims of innocence and other DNA found at the scene.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2001, British traveler Peter Falconio is presumed murdered in the Australian outback. Joanne Lees survives the attack, and Bradley John Murdoch is convicted of the murder in 2005. Peter's body is never found.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1999, NFL player Rae Carruth is convicted for orchestrating the shooting of his pregnant girlfriend. He is sentenced to 18-24 years. Their son, Chancellor, has cerebral palsy.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1975, 15-year-old Martha Moxley is murdered in Belle Haven, Connecticut. The case goes cold until 2002, when Michael Skakel, a family member, is convicted based on new evidence. Skakel maintains his innocence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2007, Meredith Kercher is murdered in Italy. Amanda Knox and Raffaele Sollecito are initially convicted but later exonerated. Rudy Guede is convicted. The Kercher family still seeks justice.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1995 Paris, Guy Georges, a serial killer, was linked to multiple murders through DNA and later confessed in court. He was sentenced to life in prison, leading to the establishment of a DNA database for offenders in France.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1990, Danny Rolling killed five students in Gainesville, Florida. He confessed to the crimes, pleaded guilty to avoid the death penalty, but was ultimately sentenced to death and executed 16 years later.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: This is the tragic tale of Susan Smith, who shocked the nation in 1994 by fabricating a carjacking to cover up the drowning murders of her two young sons.

programme.title: Naked Science: The Great Flood
programme.desc: Almost every culture on Earth tells the story of an ancient flood – details vary but the basic plot is the same - An angry God wipes out an unrepentant population.

programme.title: Naked Science: How To Avert an Asteroid Impact
programme.desc: Throughout history, the earth has been bombarded from space. Asteroid impacts have caused mass extinctions – including the dinosaur wipe out.

programme.title: Naked Science: Great Lakes
programme.desc: The Great Lakes are sometimes referred to as inland seas; they are the largest freshwater system in the world. Naked Science examines the early history of these amazing bodies

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Silencing Terry
programme.desc: Terry wants to demonstrate how Hollywood has used different objects to silence a gunshot.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Punishing Larry
programme.desc: Grenades, propane tanks, and a bathtub, all adds up to some big tests of the popular comic book character.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Leftovers Again?
programme.desc: Terry and Larry test things they never got to from other episodes. Pull up a chair and get ready for leftovers, and a surprise guest!

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Bond, Terry Bond
programme.desc: Terry and Larry will be testing the effectiveness of driving a vehicle with fully automatic machine guns mounted to the car!

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Nightmare on Hollywood Weapons
programme.desc: Terry and Larry get to test several scenes from Horror films!

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Astronauts
programme.desc: One hundred kilometres above the Earth’s surface lies an invisible frontier: the edge of space.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Getting There
programme.desc: Launching payloads into space is a very difficult task, historically rocket launches are the proverbial 'swords into ploughshares'.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Orbit
programme.desc: The International Space Station is undoubtedly the jewel in the crown of low Earth orbit; observation post, research lab and class room.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Shore To Shore
programme.desc: Our seafaring nature has translated well to the space age, seeking to sail out into the unknown in search of riches of one kind or another.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Mars: Adapt Or Leave
programme.desc: As the latest successes and failures come and go Mars continues to give up her secrets.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Emissaries
programme.desc: Our solar system is vast, from our own star the Sun to Earth is nearly 150 million kilometers or 1 Astronomical Unit.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Danger, Astreroids Ahead
programme.desc: The question often rises is the cost of space exploration really worth it? Could the money be better spent elsewhere.

programme.title: The New Frontier: First Light
programme.desc: For an instrument first developed as recently as the 17th century, the telescope has travelled a long way.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Stars Above
programme.desc: Stars are a bit like human beings. They can be warm or cold, they come in all kinds of shapes and sizes.

programme.title: Wildest Arctic: Taiga: The Frozen Forest
programme.desc: Containing one third of the world's trees, the frozen forests of the Taiga are the lungs of the planet. This great forest spans across shores of the Pacific, sheltering some of the Earth's most majestic animals, and some of its most secretive.

programme.title: Wildest Indonesia
programme.desc: How have Indonesia’s remote and mysterious islands become home to so many of the natural world’s most extraordinary giants? From a unique species of elephant, and the world’s longest snake, to a crab almost a metre wide that climbs trees

programme.title: Jungle Planet
programme.desc: The rivers of the Northern Hemisphere are naturally flanked by forests adapted to periodic flooding.

programme.title: Jungle Planet
programme.desc: African rainforests are the oldest in the world. They are inhabited by some of the most legendary animals, such as gorillas, chimpanzees and okapi.

programme.title: Wildest Latin America: The Andes: World in the Clouds
programme.desc: The longest chain of mountains in the world, the Andes run the length of western South America and dictate the climate for the whole continent. They include some of the highest peaks outside the Himalayas.

programme.title: Meerkat Manor: Rise of the Dynasty
programme.desc: Sencha’s return threatens Flint’s pups, and a storm leaves Swift down on numbers and Pandi with an opportunity.

programme.title: Witches: A Century of Murder: Episode 2

programme.title: Britain's Bloody Crown

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Once Johannesburg was the most dangerous city in the world, but the end of the apartheid has lead to vast changes in the city. Tony visits the Johannesburg suburb of Hillbrow, and discovers the culture and, of course food.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Bourdain looks at the Congo as one of the more industrialized powers in Africa now largely crumbled to nothingness and chaos with Tim Butcher author of Blood River.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown: Salvador, Brazil

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: Arthur: D.W. Goes To Washington/Arthur's Mystery Envelope
programme.desc: D.W.'s on her way to Washington and she's bored by it all until she gets lost during a tour of the White House. Then, when Mr. Haney gives Arthur a sealed envelope to bring home to Mom, Arthur fears the worst.

programme.title: Arthur: D.W. All Wet/Buster's Dino Dilemma
programme.desc: The Reads are having a wonderful time at the beach, except for D.W. who simply refuses to have fun. Next, Buster and Arthur make a long-awaited field trip to a dinosaur excavation.

programme.title: Arthur: Grandma Thora Appreciation Day/Fern's Slumber Party
programme.desc: Arthur and D.W. are planning a surprise party for Grandma Thora. But amidst the party-planning mayhem, they forget about the guest of honor! Then, shy Fern hosts a slumber party at her mother's urging.

programme.title: Arthur: Making Conversation/A Cloudy Day
programme.desc: Talking to adults makes George want to hide! When he learns he must go to a grown-up party, he turns to his friends for help. Then, Arthur and his friends learn that old silent films can be just as much fun as the movies they’re used to.

programme.title: Arthur: Pick a Car, Any Car/Jenna's Bedtime Blues
programme.desc: The family car is broken, and so is Arthur's heart. How can Mom and Dad even think of replacing it? It’s Muffy's annual slumber party, and Jenna is finally invited! But what if the other girls discover that she's an occasional bed-wetter?

programme.title: Arthur: Buster's Back/The Ballad Of Buster Baxter
programme.desc: Buster's coming home! The word spreads fast, but so does the fear that Buster will be different. Then, it seems to Buster, not only have his friends changed while he was gone, but they've forgotten all about him! Will he ever fit in again?

programme.title: Arthur: The Last Day (Part 1 & 2)
programme.desc: It's a time of change at Lakewood Elementary as Arthur and Buster move up to 4th grade. D.W. and Bud prepare to enter kindergarten and Muffy and Francine contemplate how spending the summer away from each other will affect their friendship.

programme.title: Arthur: Bully for Binky/Misfortune Teller
programme.desc: Is Binky Barnes really the most disgusting, nasty, horrible person on the planet, or is he just misunderstood? Next, what's got four sides and the answer to everything? Prunella's cootie catcher!

programme.title: Arthur: All About D.W./Blockheads
programme.desc: When Emily gets the starring role in the preschool play, D.W. is beside herself with jealousy. She should be Little Red Riding Hood, not Tree #2! Then, D.W. and Emily are upset when they get stuck with the blocks at playtime.

programme.title: RAF: The Royal Air Force: Battle for the Skies
programme.desc: By the end of the war the RAF had aircraft that could escort bombers to targets deep inside Germany and back. With the Gloster Meteor, the RAF had also entered the ‘Jet Age’. Aircraft like the Hawker Hunter and F-86 Sabre took the RAF supersonic.

programme.title: RAF: The Royal Air Force: Battle for the Skies
programme.desc: By the end of the First World War the RAF had bombers that could fly to Berlin and back. For the first time in a thousand years British civilians found themselves under direct attack - by Zeppelin airships and Gotha bombers.

programme.title: RAF: The Royal Air Force: Battle for the Skies
programme.desc: The RAF’s maritime role is often overlooked. Between the wars the RAF operated elegant flying boats to patrol the far flung corners of the Empire.

programme.title: RAF: The Royal Air Force: Battle for the Skies
programme.desc: After the war, RAF transports helped keep Berliners alive during the Soviet blockade of the city in 1948/49. As well as flying round-the-clock operations into the airport, Sunderland flying boats delivered supplies by landing on Lake Havel near the city.

programme.title: RAF: The Royal Air Force: Battle for the Skies
programme.desc: In this programme we bring the RAF story into the modern era. Another aircraft to have extended the RAF’s capabilities is the C130 Hercules. This aircraft has proved its capabilities in countless environments from resupply, to dropping paratroopers.

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Discover the true story of a German Luftwaffe pilot in World War II who risked his own life to save the crew of an American B-17 bomber.

programme.title: U-Boat Wargamers: The Wrens
programme.desc: When Gilbert Roberts arrives to set up a tactical school for the U-boats, he receives a frosty reception from Admiral Noble. Enlisting the help of Jean Laidlaw and a small team of Wrens, Roberts begins unpicking the flaws in Walker’s ‘Buttercup’.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Expeditions: Into the Mountains (Tibet)

programme.title: Married to Medicine
programme.desc: When her ailing father visits, Contessa discovers his health is even worse than she thought; Mariah has a real talk with Lauren about growing up and getting ready for high school; Simone faces a hard talk with her son about the state of her marriage.

programme.title: Married to Medicine
programme.desc: Things continue to heat up at the crab boil when Heavenly and Mariah's feud reaches a fever pitch; Quad opens up to the group; Contessa has a conversation with her father about a life-altering decision; Dr. K encourages Heavenly to dig into her past.

programme.title: Married to Medicine
programme.desc: Quad has a heart-to-heart conversation with Jackie over her marital issues; the guys have a much-needed night to themselves during which Greg tells his side of the story; with Simone and Jackie in tow, Heavenly travels to her childhood home in Miami.

programme.title: Married to Medicine
programme.desc: Heavenly continues her spiritual journey in Miami; Contessa goes through with her double mastectomy; Quad turns to old friends for comfort; Toya and Eugene appreciate the progress of their dream home; Mariah and Aydin explain the birds and the bees.

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing New York
programme.desc: When Fredrik gets a 17 million dollar Tribeca townhouse, Ryan sabotages his open house by jumping into the swimming pool; new agent, Luis D. Ortiz, tries to make a mark in Manhattan by finding an unlisted, off-market property for a potential client.

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing New York
programme.desc: When Ryan and Luis list units in the same building, things get competitive; Fredrik is struggling to sell the Marble House and just when he's got a lead, Hurricane Sandy strikes.

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing New York
programme.desc: Luis gets the first townhouse of his young career, but is horrified to find that most of the decor is older than he is; Ryan deals with a picky senior citizen.

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing New York
programme.desc: Fredrik and Luis have listing opportunities for new developments on the same exact street, but only one of these agents escapes making any commission; Ryan's new client forces him to be more creative than ever.

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing New York
programme.desc: Luis' client needs to sell her apartment quickly; Ryan tries to calm his client; Fredrik gets another listing.

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing New York
programme.desc: Fredrik and Luis bond over drinks, and Fredrik shares some valuable information; Ryan tries to find an apartment for an Idaho housewife.

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster
programme.desc: The pilot of Swissair Flight 111 smells smoke in the cockpit. Minutes later fire and complete electrical failure cause the MD11 to crash into the Atlantic killing all 229 passengers and crew on board.

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster
programme.desc: Aeroperu Flight 603 bound from Lima, Peru to Santiago, Chile shows how a simple error and a lack of flight crew training can bring a giant, complex airliner to a deadly end.

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster
programme.desc: Shortly after Air Alaska Flight 261 takes off, the stabilizer malfunctions. The pilots struggle to control the plane and it dives into the ocean. The eighty-eight passengers and crew on board die.

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster
programme.desc: An Air Transat Airbus runs out of fuel over the Atlantic and has to glide to a power-free landing.

programme.title: Mayday Air Disaster
programme.desc: The windshield of British Airways Flight 111 has popped off and the captain has been sucked out. The sound of the wind is deafening and the co-pilot can’t hear the replies from air traffic controllers

programme.title: A New Life In The Sun
programme.desc: Time is running out for one man to make a profit in St Tropez. In Spain, a British couple need to bring in more euros by opening a restaurant—but can they get a licence? And in France, can one expat cope with running the bed & breakfast on his own?

programme.title: A New Life In The Sun
programme.desc: Has one expat become the victim of his own success in the Cote d’Azur? The heat causes havoc with the floor in a French bed & breakfast, and in Spain, there is good news in for a new restaurant in Almeria.

programme.title: A New Life In The Sun
programme.desc: In Valencia, a couple try pushing their bike business to the next level. The expat owners of a bed & breakfast end their season busier than ever, and two former teachers take on their biggest booking yet.

programme.title: A New Life In The Sun
programme.desc: A mother and daughter team with a dream of running a home furnishing shop in the Costa Blanca. A couple swaps Harlow with the French countryside and a future as holiday homeowners, and a yoga teacher from Somerset tests out a Spanish villa.

programme.title: A New Life In The Sun
programme.desc: A yoga teacher has a rude awakening in Spain. In France, the British owners of a gite welcome their biggest booking, and there’s drama in the Costa Blanca as the new owners find out they won’t receive their merchandise in time for opening.

programme.title: A New Life In The Sun
programme.desc: In the Costa Blanca, a family of expats moves into their new home. In France, two gite owners race against time to renovate their barn. And a worrying start in business for a Somerset yoga teacher whose dream to run retreats hangs in the balance.

programme.title: Magi-Nation

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: Jet’s skateboard prowess lands him an endorsement deal with a local extreme sports company. The terms of the deal with “Gnarly Sports” requires him to wear the Gnarly Logo on everything, including his Posse gear and weapons.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: McGee the Mermaid / Welcome to Buttcon
programme.desc: The kids accidentally resurrect an evil mermaid who floods the camp.

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse

programme.desc: The Campers and Buttsquat come across an ancient pyramid inhabited by mummy's.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: On Again, Off Again

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Tea-riffic Manners

programme.title: Barney and Friends: All Aboard

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Pistachio - BJ Snack Attack
programme.desc: Barney is snacking on pistachios...and just can't seem to stop! His young friends try to help by hiding them from Barney. When that doesn't work, they tell him a story to help him stop eating pistachios.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: A New Friend
programme.desc: While Barney has fun with his friends every day, there's something special about making a brand NEW friend! When Mr. Boyd's niece, Colleen visits the park, she's eager to make friends with the other kids...but being the "new kid" isn't easy.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Hot Rodding Teenagers from Dimension X
programme.desc: Shredder experiments with the Inter-Dimensional Portal, hoping to contact Krang's' Rock Soldiers and prepare an invasion of Earth.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Shredder & Splintered
programme.desc: Shredder lures Splinter into the Technodrome, this time with the promise that a weapon in his possession can restore the sensei to human form.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Return of the Shredder
programme.desc: Shredder trains students at the Dojo for a crooked Turtles gang. The mock turtles' crime spree starts turning the public against Michelangelo, Raphael, Donatello and Leonardo.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Incredible Shrinking Turtles
programme.desc: In the first of a four-parter, the amazing amphibians search for the segments of the powerful crystal Eye of Sarnoth. Shredder cuts the Turtles down to size, literally.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): It Came from Beneath the Sewers
programme.desc: In the second of a four-parter about the pursuit of the Eye of Sarnoth, the Turtles are attacked by a huge alien plant that is making New York into a jungle.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Mean Machines
programme.desc: The hunt for the Eye of Sarnoth continues in the third of a four-parter with the Turtles in Groucho-like disguises. Shredder hooks up a fragment of the Eye to the computer Omnus.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Curse of the Evil Eye
programme.desc: Shredder is in control of the Eye of Sarnoth, enabling him to transmute matter. Shredder brings to life a dragon which soars through the air with shocked shellbacks Leonardo and Michelangelo aboard.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Case of the Killer Pizzas
programme.desc: The Turtles' favourite food, pizza, is also the source of their problem. Nasty, fanged creatures have been planted in pizza boxes! When the critters crack open their chrysalises, they become enormous.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Enter: The Fly
programme.desc: Shredder sends April a rare duko plant that renders her unconscious. The Turtles seek a rare leaf that can serve as an antidote.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Invasion of the Punk Frogs
programme.desc: Mutagen, sent by Krang from Dimension X, changes four Florida frogs into half-human creatures four and a half feet tall. Shredder, training them in his evil ways, names them Attila the Frog, Genghis Frog, Rasputin the Mad Frog and Napoleon Bonafrog.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Splinter No More
programme.desc: Michelangelo senses that their mentor is "mondo depressed" and Donatello finds a way of changing back Splinter the rat to Yoshi the man.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Jon decides to buy a cleaning robot because of Garfield and Odie's filthiness. The robot soon becomes very violent around Garfield, Odie and Jon.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Jon puts Garfield on a diet and forces him to exercise, when the scales tell him that he's overweight.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Jon has difficulty sleeping, so a hypnotist puts Jon under hypnosis making him fall asleep from the noise of a horn, but he accidentally responds to Odie's bark instead.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield accidentally gets sent into a parallel world; Garfield gets a taste of celebrity and glitter.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield and Odie are framed for stealing barbecued ribs when they run away from Jon's twin girl cousins.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield is suddenly sucked through a vortex into a parallel universe where cats' and humans' roles are reversed.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield is enrolled on the Dr Whipple's Pet obedience course after Jon sneakily tapes him misbehaving.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: When an alien stops at a supermarket on earth, Garfield mistakenly enters his spaceship and travels through different planets, but it becomes hard for him to survive.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Sir Leo, who is also Liz's cat, claims that the life of a superstar is great. Sir Leo looks just like Garfield but speaks with a British accent.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield and Odie discover talking fish in the sea.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: When Jon agrees to look after Liz's fish, Garfield can't resist eating them.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: When Garfield, Jon, Odie and Liz go on a picnic, Odie sneaks a baby wolf into the house and sends the mum after Jon and Garfield.

programme.title: The Garfield Show
programme.desc: Garfield finds a Pocket watch which can stop time.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings prepare a dream afternoon snack with the last jar of chocolate spread in the cupboard. Tough luck, Grizzy is coming. Caught off guard, the Lemmings hide the jar in a teddy bear-pyjama case to slip away without arousing suspicion.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy gleefully discovers that he can print images taken with the ranger's camera as it's connected to a state-of-the-art printer. He decides to frame himself, but when the time comes to print his portrait, there's no more paper in the printer.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: A van pulls up to the ranger's cabin to deliver a full crate of jars of chocolate spread. While Grizzy and the Lemmings fight over a wrecking bar to open it, someone steals the crate.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: As usual, Grizzly picks the door of the Ranger's log cabin to come in and make himself comfortable, but this time it's impossible to enter. The Lemmings have requisitioned the cabin for a party and barricaded themselves inside.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have got their hands on some white beans which have very strange properties, when mixed with soda anyone who eats them turns into a bouncing ball. Grizzy realises that if he wants to have a nap, he must get rid of the magic beans.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly wants to get rid of the old piano in the garage at any cost, for the Lemmings love using it to make a dissonant racket. But when he notices a she-bear he's in love with likes the melody of Fur Elise, he decides to learn the piece.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings inherit a crate that fell off a Canadian army convoy transporting ultra-secret material. The case inside the crate emits a sort of laser beam that divides objects into multiple miniaturised versions of themselves.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Near the supermarket, a machine hands out surprise eggs in exchange for a coin. Amongst the lots to be won, there is a super firework rocket the Lemmings dream of.

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: The gang discovers a large group of wild Pikachu, and though they first seem frightened by the intruder, the group eventually opens up to Ash’s Pikachu. How will Pikachu feel seeing others of its kind in the wild?

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Ash and his friends find a large home with a lavish garden party in full swing, attended by the Trainers of Pokémon who evolve using Evolution stones. Maybe they can see the seemingly abandoned Eevee’s Trainer here?

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
